title: hop_limit not decremented for forwarded packets
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:259-347

The Ipv6Header parsing and serialization code preserves hop_limit as-is. While the module explicitly states it doesn't handle routing, any future forwarding use would need to:

1. Decrement hop_limit before forwarding
2. Drop packets with hop_limit=0 (send ICMPv6 Time Exceeded)

Currently neither is implemented. If this module is used in a forwarding context without modification, it could create routing loops.

This is documented as out-of-scope ('no routing tables'), but worth noting for anyone extending the module.

Suggestion: Add a comment in Ipv6Header noting that forwarding implementations MUST decrement hop_limit, or add a decrement_hop_limit() method that returns Option<Self> (None if already 0).
close_reason: Fixed off-by-one in with_decremented_hop_limit (now correctly returns None when decrement reaches zero per RFC 8200 §3); added independent test vector exercising preservation of other header fields, hop=5->4, and zero/1 cases. Updated doc comment. Only touched specified file. Test and logic verified.
